mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, the cost of capital for equities rises sharply, compressing forward earnings valuations. This elevated rate regime produces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors reprice equity risk premiums downward.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. This contraction in employment growth predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations fall.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of months where PAYEMS declines month-over-month, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and forward SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal robust consumer confidence and construction-sector demand. This demand expansion drives positive equity returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate higher corporate earnings from construction, materials, and related sectors.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with expanding housing demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST rises > 5% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth combined with stable inflation signals healthy supply-side expansion without cost-push pressures. This productive capacity growth supports margin expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5% MoM AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month change < 0.3%
regime_hint: goldilocks expansion with supply growth and contained inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of instances where both conditions are met, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth decelerates sharply while unemployment remains elevated, signaling weakening consumer demand and labor market stress. This dual deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings guidance falls.
regime_hint: demand-side recession with labor market weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where both conditions hold, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate falls below 3.7%, indicating a tight labor market with wage pressure and reduced slack. This tight labor condition produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors anticipate Fed tightening and margin compression.
regime_hint: tight labor market with inflation risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE < 3.7%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation measured by CPIAUCSL declines month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains above 4.5%, the real interest rate rises sharply, signaling monetary policy tightness. This real rate shock produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where both conditions are met, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 accelerates above trend while retail sales RSAFS expand, signaling robust demand-side momentum. This synchronized demand expansion produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts rise.
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of instances where both GDPC1 and RSAFS show positive momentum, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
